item.title: Why Voters Might Finally Blame Trump for Rising Costs
item.description: Trump’s Iran war will affect more than just gas prices.

item.title: Trump Has Declared War on Cheap Gas. Again.
item.description: The administration’s attacks on renewable energy mean higher costs for everyone.

item.title: Carolyn Bessette Was Living the Dream. Then She Met John.
item.description: The fairy tale was 1990s New York.

item.title: Trump Is the Anti-Trump
item.description: There is an alternate universe in which the president is the popular, successful figure of his imagination.

item.title: A Kurdish Rebellion in Iran Could Be a Disaster
item.description: The United States and Israel have considered using Iranian Kurds as a ground force.

item.title: Latin Americans Already Have a Serious Partner — and It’s Not Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to force the Western Hemisphere into alignment will keep falling short.

item.title: The Smash-and-Grab Presidency Reaches Its Apex
item.description: When disruption becomes destruction.

item.title: They Feel Bugs Inside Them. Doctors Don’t Know What They Are Either.
item.description: Delusional infestation is one of the darkest embodiments of medical uncertainty.

item.title: How Does This End? Four Scenarios for What Comes Next With Iran.
item.description: The best hope? Weaken the regime’s ability to lash out and resist an uprising.

item.title: Hopes for Civility in the Texas Senate Race
item.description: Readers respond to a David French column on James Talarico, a Christian Democrat running for the Senate in Texas. Also: Good health and common sense; the upside of small talk.

item.title: I Asked a Former Trump Official to Justify This War
item.description: Nadia Schadlow, a deputy national security adviser during Trump’s first term, explains how the U.S.-Israel war with Iran fits in with an “America first” agenda.

item.title: The Popcorn Resistance of ‘One Battle After Another’
item.description: My parents were radical. Paul Thomas Anderson’s film is anything but.

item.title: How Trump Betrayed His First Duty to Americans
item.description: The Trump administration failed to plan for evacuating either its diplomats or its citizens and did not adequately warn them.

item.title: A.I. Is Changing the Way We Think About Good and Evil
item.description: Chatbots can teach us about human nature.

item.title: If Trump’s Allies Win, Red State Governance Will Look Just Like D.C. Chaos
item.description: Old guard Republicans have maintained good governance in many red states, but MAGA chaos is coming for them, too.

item.title: Early Telephones Were Scary, but No One Panicked
item.description: The early telephone didn’t spark panic. That doesn’t mean we’re wrong to panic about smartphones.

item.title: It’s Trump’s War. We’re Stuck With the Bill.
item.description: What are we getting for all of President Trump’s military spending?

item.title: It’s Oscars Snubbing Season Again
item.description: In a culture that can’t concentrate, perhaps our fixation on Oscar snubs is healthy.

item.title: Why You Should Read Adam Smith
item.description: The news is good. Really.

item.title: Trump Has No Idea How to End the War With Iran
item.description: Only when the U.S.-Israeli bombing of Iran comes to a halt, without destroying the state, can the Iranian people sort out their fate.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Represents a Choice: Defiance
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader has been selected not to break with the regime but to preserve it.

item.title: James Talarico Is a Christian X-Ray
item.description: The Democratic Senate candidate in Texas is teaching us about what faith really means.

item.title: Fears of a ‘Quagmire’ in Trump’s War on Iran
item.description: As Iran chooses another hard-line leader, readers express anxiety about the war and its consequences.

item.title: Amid the Iran War, the U.S. Must Cut Its Exposure to Oil Price Spikes
item.description: America is a big oil producer. But its overreliance on crude and the president’s efforts to keep it that way mean times like these can hurt.

item.title: Kristi Noem’s Tenure Illustrates Trump’s Unserious White House
item.description: Kristi Noem epitomizes the incompetence of this White House.

item.title: Daryl Hannah: How Can ‘Love Story’ Get Away With This?
item.description: The character “Daryl Hannah” in “Love Story” is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life or my conduct — and these kinds of lies don’t go away.

item.title: The Danger of Being a Palestinian Citizen of Israel
item.description: The Israeli government must address a crime wave in Palestinian towns.

item.title: The Future We Feared Is Already Here
item.description: The showdown between the Pentagon and Anthropic is a window into how unprepared we are for the questions we are facing.

item.title: Are Declining Birthrates Really a Problem?
item.description: What do declining birthrates really mean for the future? Readers respond to a front-page news article.

item.title: Was Tyra Banks the Villain? Or Were We?
item.description: A fashion insider and two culture writers dissect “America’s Next Top Model.”

item.title: The Badlands Hold Me as I Grieve
item.description: I imagine the birds I see are the family members I’ve lost.

item.title: The ‘Arrogance of Power’ Drives War in Iran
item.description: The Iranian people are not overthrowing their government, and the war is spreading across the region.

item.title: Going to War With Iran, Surrounded by Yes Men
item.description: Members of President Trump’s cabinet see their jobs as “total obedience to the president’s will,” the columnist Jamelle Bouie argues, and that means we should all be very worried about how the war in Iran could unfold.

item.title: One President’s Whim. A World in Crisis.
item.description: A week into Trump’s war in Iran, his strategy is still a mystery.

item.title: What Watching a Furious Hillary Clinton Showed Me
item.description: Her exasperation during her Epstein-related deposition channeled the national mood for some of us.

item.title: There Is One Crucial Reason We’re Talking About Boots on the Ground
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have no good options to address an unavoidable problem.

item.title: ‘Wuthering Heights,’ MAGA Style
item.description: Kristi and Corey cavorted in the swamp as D.H.S. got bogged down.

item.title: Trump’s Iran War and the Shadow of Iraq
item.description: The desire for a revolutionary presidency gives hubris a lot of space to work.

item.title: How Trump Views War
item.description: President Trump’s war in Iran isn’t so much a strategy as a game of “Risk,” says the Times Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie. On “The Opinions” podcast, he explains that the administration sees the world as “we big, they small,” with other nations as non-playable characters reacting to America, the “protagonists of reality.”

item.title: What Does School Choice Really Mean?
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay that argued that more school vouchers would improve public education.

item.title: One President’s Whim. A World in Crisis.
item.description: A week into Trump’s war in Iran, his strategy is still a mystery.

item.title: I’ve Never Been a Patriot, but Spain Standing Up to Trump Has Made Me One
item.description: There are times when it is clear what it means to stand up for what is right.

item.title: Trump’s Removal of Noem
item.description: Readers respond to the dismissal of Kristi Noem as the homeland security secretary. Also: Two ways of parking; Meta’s facial recognition.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein Was Wicked, but as the Files Show, He Was Also Banal
item.description: Just look at his philosophical opinions.

item.title: Why the Pentagon Wants to Destroy Anthropic
item.description: The former A.I. policy adviser to the Trump White House explains why the conflict between Anthropic and the White House is so dangerous.
